Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function and possible tubular damage. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, arguing against diabetes. The findings may indicate acute or chronic kidney injury with possible hepatic involvement. Further nephrological and hepatological evaluation is recommended.
